DoubleML DiD wrappers (linear / ranger / xgboost nuisances)

Source: R/DMLearner.R
doubleml_did_linear.Rd

Estimates a first-difference outcome \(Y = Y_1 - Y_0\) with DoubleML. Binary treatment uses DoubleML::DoubleMLIRM with score = "ATTE" (default). Multi-arm discrete (more than two levels, encoded as integers) is expanded to dummies versus the smallest level and estimated with DoubleML::DoubleMLPLR. User-supplied dummy matrices or continuous \(D\) use DoubleMLPLR. Defaults follow the usual DiD + DML mapping (see Callaway & Sant'Anna 2021; DoubleML documentation).

Usage

doubleml_did_linear(
  y1,
  y0,
  D,
  covariates,
  ml_g = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_l = NULL,
  n_folds = 10L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "ATTE",
  return_ml_object = FALSE,
  ...
)

doubleml_did_rf(
  y1,
  y0,
  D,
  covariates,
  ml_g = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_l = NULL,
  n_folds = 10L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "ATTE",
  return_ml_object = FALSE,
  ...
)

doubleml_did_xgboost(
  y1,
  y0,
  D,
  covariates,
  ml_g = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_l = NULL,
  n_folds = 10L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "ATTE",
  return_ml_object = FALSE,
  ...
)

doubleml_did_eval_linear(
  y1,
  y0,
  D,
  covariates,
  ml_g = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_l = NULL,
  n_folds = 10L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "ATTE",
  print_eval = TRUE,
  return_ml_object = FALSE,
  ...
)

doubleml_did_eval_rf(
  y1,
  y0,
  D,
  covariates,
  ml_g = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_l = NULL,
  n_folds = 10L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "ATTE",
  print_eval = TRUE,
  return_ml_object = FALSE,
  ...
)

doubleml_did_eval_xgboost(
  y1,
  y0,
  D,
  covariates,
  ml_g = NULL,
  ml_m = NULL,
  ml_l = NULL,
  n_folds = 10L,
  n_rep = 1L,
  score = "ATTE",
  print_eval = TRUE,
  return_ml_object = FALSE,
  ...
)

Arguments

y1, y0

post and pre outcomes (same length).

D

treatment: vector (binary, discrete multi-arm, or continuous), or matrix of treatment columns / dummies (ncol > 1 uses PLR on all columns).

covariates

matrix or data.frame of pre-treatment covariates.

ml_g, ml_m, ml_l

mlr3 learners; if NULL, sensible defaults for the wrapper.

n_folds, n_rep

passed to DoubleML (see note on n_rep).

score

"ATTE" or "ATE" for the IRM branch only.

return_ml_object

if TRUE, include dml_obj in the return list.

...

passed to DoubleMLIRM$new or DoubleMLPLR$new.

print_eval

if TRUE, print nuisance diagnostics from doubleml_did_eval_preds.

Value

List with coef, se, psi, mode, D_model, and ATT / att.inf.func (binary IRM) or inf_func (PLR). Eval variants add eval_predictions.

Object returned by doubleml_did_rf.

Object returned by doubleml_did_xgboost.

Object returned by doubleml_did_eval_linear.

Object returned by doubleml_did_eval_rf.

Object returned by doubleml_did_eval_xgboost.

References

Callaway & Sant'Anna (2021); Bach et al. (2021) doi:10.48550/arXiv.2103.09603 ; DoubleML https://docs.doubleml.org/

See also

RCausalML-package

RCausalML-package

RCausalML-package

RCausalML-package

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_did_linear(...)
} # }
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_did_rf(...)
} # }
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_did_xgboost(...)
} # }
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_did_eval_linear(...)
} # }
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_did_eval_rf(...)
} # }
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# doubleml_did_eval_xgboost(...)
} # }
